title: Medicine's Deadliest Problem Doesn't Appear on Death Certificates
description: Medical error kills somewhere between 250,000 and 795,000 Americans every year — and almost none of those deaths are officially recorded as such. This piece investigates why the U.S. death-coding system has no category for human or system failure, what two decades of patient safety reform have actually achieved, and why diagnostic error in particular remains a largely unaddressed public health catastrophe.
